findings.title: Sequential Šidák (m = spent+1) does NOT control family-wise false-PASS at 0.05 — measured FWER = 20.6% at the 50-test budget (4.1× target); the docstring/config claim of FWER control is false
findings.detail: campaign_adjusted_t judges test i at alpha_i = 1-(1-0.05)^(1/m) with m = terminal-tests-already-spent + 1 = the test's POSITION i. Under independent nulls the realized family-wise false-PASS probability is 1 - Π_{i=1}^{N}(1-alpha_i) = 1 - 0.95^(H_N). Numerically (verified with scipy): N=5 -> 0.1105, N=10 -> 0.1395, N=20 -> 0.1685, N=50 -> 0.2061. So at the campaign budget the false-PASS rate is 20.6%, ~4.1x the nominal CAMPAIGN_ALPHA_FAMILY=0.05, and it RISES with lineage count — the opposite of the spec's acceptance criterion (COVERAGE-GATE-SAFETY-MECHANISMS.md line 231: 'campaign-wide false OOS PASS rate stays at the target as lineage count scales'). The service.py docstring literally asserts 'the family-wise false-PASS rate across the campaign's spent tests stays at CAMPAIGN_ALPHA_FAMILY' — this is provably false. This is the SAME non-controlling 'online per-peek escalation' the codebase's own _sidak_t_star already documents as ~11-17% not 5% (loop.py:455-461), re-applied to FB4 but mislabeled as family-wise control. The honest scheme that actually holds FWER<=0.05 is a FIXED bar at m = BUDGET = 50 (every test faces norm.ppf(1-(1-0.05)^(1/50)) ~ 3.083): measured FWER = 0.0500 at N=50 and <=0.05 for all N<50. The spec is internally inconsistent (line 229 offers 'an alpha that shrinks as cumulative evals grow' as an option) but the binding acceptance test (231) plus the doc's stated ethos of rounding in the stricter direction (line 28) demand true control -> fixed m=BUDGET, not m=spent+1. As written FB4 does not deliver the multiplicity control that the whole RT2/soft-DSR relocation-of-risk argument depends on.

findings.title: Campaign ledger is per-run ephemeral in the default/production path (oos_db_path=':memory:'), so the 'campaign-wide / per-user' cap and Šidák escalation reset to zero every run — restarting runs is a full reset (order + budget), directly gameable
findings.detail: run_research defaults oos_db_path=':memory:' and the server router (_run_and_track) calls run_research WITHOUT overriding it, so the OOSLockboxService — including the new OOSCampaignRow ledger — lives in an in-memory SQLite DB created fresh per run_research call and discarded at run end. Consequences: (1) The per-user scope_key = str(user_id) is inert across runs; tests_terminal restarts at 0 on every run, so every run's FIRST terminal OOS test faces the 1.645 (alpha .05) bar again and the 50-cap never accumulates across runs. (2) This is exactly the 'restart runs to reset order' gameability under review point (b): there is no reset PATH to guard because there is no persistence at all by default — a user running 50 one-candidate runs faces alpha .05 every time and never depletes the budget. (3) It contradicts the design claims that gate this batch: config JSON says 'ledger persisted in oos_lockbox.db (oos_campaign_ledger)' and 'volume can never buy more lottery tickets'; the batch note asserts 'the ledger persists across runs per scope'. In the shipped default it does not. FB4 only controls multiplicity WITHIN a single run_research invocation. Either the default must be a persistent file DB for the per-user scope to mean anything, or the per-user scoping + 'campaign-wide across runs' + 'persisted' claims must be dropped and FB4 documented as within-run only. Minor related nit: the bar is read (loop.py:729) in a separate Session from the atomic increment in evaluate(); concurrent same-scope runs can read a stale count and be judged at too-low a bar (the hard cap itself stays atomic).

findings.title: RT2 drops the spec-required MON1 'canary-proven-vacuous' precondition — soft_dsr softens DSR on any robustness+OOS run regardless of whether DSR is actually vacuous, and its FB4 backstop leaks at 20.6%, reproducing the net-loosening the spec forbids
findings.code_ref: src/backend/ai/research/run.py:308 (_soft_dsr = soft_dsr and enable_oos and mode=='robustness'); gatekeeper.py:89-95; spec lines 92/111/175
findings.detail: COVERAGE-GATE-SAFETY-MECHANISMS.md states in three places (lines 92, 111, 175) that DSR softens to SOFT ONLY when ALL THREE hold: oos_enabled=True AND the OOS stage is multiplicity-aware (FB4) AND the power canary MON1 has PROVEN the gate vacuous. The implementation's guard checks only two (enable_oos AND mode==robustness); the MON1 canary-vacuity precondition is entirely absent, so an operator can soft-DSR on any robustness run whether or not DSR is saturated. The guard chokepoint itself is otherwise complete (only run.py:309 builds a gatekeeper with soft_dsr; regime keeps DSR HARD via the elif in gatekeeper.py:83-95; default off; API field default False), so there is no unguarded softening PATH in-tree. But because the FB4 backstop it relocates risk onto does not control FWER (finding #1: 20.6% vs 5%), enabling soft_dsr moves the one in-sample multiplicity gate's decision onto an OOS stage that leaks at ~4x target — precisely the 'relocate best-of-many-tries risk onto a stage that does not control it — a net loosening' that spec line 111 says RT2 may not do until FB3+FB4 genuinely control it. RT2 should stay unavailable (or DSR stay HARD) until #1 is fixed and the MON1 precondition is wired.

findings.title: The spec-mandated FWER simulation acceptance test is missing — the new suite only checks monotonicity and point t* values, so the 20.6% miscontrol ships green
findings.detail: Spec line 231 explicitly requires: 'Simulation that campaign-wide false OOS PASS rate stays at the target as lineage count scales.' The new test file has no such simulation — test_sidak_bar_is_monotone_and_floored only asserts the bars increase and match hand-computed points (1.645/2.568/3.083), and test_adjusted_t_star_tightens_the_oos_verdict only checks a single candidate flips PASS->not-PASS. None of them exercise the family-wise error rate across N nulls, which is the actual FB4 guarantee. A ~10-line Monte-Carlo (draw N null t-stats, apply the sequential bars, count families with >=1 PASS) would have surfaced finding #1 immediately (empirical ~0.20 at N=50 vs asserted 0.05). This is the difference between pinning the mechanism's constants and pinning its statistical claim.

findings.title: Within-run order-dependence: first candidate faces alpha .05 (1.645), 50th faces alpha ~.001 (3.08) — a favored candidate can be handed the easy bar by submitting it first
findings.code_ref: src/backend/ai/research/loop.py:727-730 (bar read from current count); service.py:128
findings.detail: Because the per-test bar is a function of arrival order (m = tests spent so far + 1), the first candidate evaluated in a run always gets the loosest 1.645 bar and later ones get progressively stricter bars up to ~3.08. The aggregate family FWER is order-invariant (product is commutative -> fixed 20.6% for N tests), so this is 'acceptable-sequential' for the family total, but it does let an operator route their preferred hypothesis to the cheapest bar by ordering it first within a run. In isolation this is a minor fairness/forking-path concern; combined with finding #2 (:memory: reset every run) it becomes fully gameable — every run resets the order so the easy 1.645 bar is always available on demand. A fixed m=BUDGET bar (finding #1's fix) also eliminates this order-dependence.

findings.title: Floor constant 1.645 (service) vs VALIDATE_T 1.65 (loop) mismatch — harmless due to the >VALIDATE_T guard but the config docstring conflates the two
findings.code_ref: service.py:130 (max(..., 1.645)); loop.py:268 (VALIDATE_T=1.65), :575; config JSON 'floored at the single-test 1.645'
findings.detail: campaign_adjusted_t floors at 1.645 while loop.VALIDATE_T is 1.65, and _oos_verdict honors t_star only when t_star > VALIDATE_T (loop.py:575). So campaign_adjusted_t(0)=1.645 is correctly IGNORED (not > 1.65) and the verdict uses 1.65 — the direction is right (t_star can only RAISE the bar, never loosen below the single-test floor; verified by the 1.0 case returning PASS). No behavioral bug. But the config JSON and service docstring describe the floor as 'the single-test 1.645' / 'the single-test VALIDATE_T' as if these are the same number; they differ by 0.005. Cosmetic — align the constants (use VALIDATE_T) so the freeze/tamper test and the two floors reference one source of truth.

result.summary: FB4's core statistical claim is false and is the blocker. The sequential Šidák bar (m = tests-already-spent + 1) does NOT control family-wise false-PASS at 0.05: verified numerically the realized FWER = 1 - 0.95^(H_N) = 0.2061 at the 50-test budget (4.1x target), rising with lineage count — the exact opposite of the spec's acceptance criterion (line 231). It is the same non-controlling 'online escalation' the codebase's own _sidak_t_star already documents as ~11-17%, re-applied but mislabeled as FWER control in the service.py docstring and config JSON. The honest scheme that actually holds <=0.05 is a FIXED bar at m = BUDGET = 50 (measured FWER = 0.0500), which the doc's stricter-direction ethos + binding acceptance test demand. Second, the 'campaign-wide, per-user, persisted' ledger is a fiction in the default/production path: oos_db_path defaults to ':memory:' and the router never overrides it, so the ledger AND the 50-cap reset to zero every run — restarting runs fully resets order and budget (gameable; contradicts 'volume can never buy more lottery tickets'). FB4 only controls multiplicity within a single run. Third, RT2 drops the spec-required MON1 canary-vacuity precondition, so soft_dsr can relocate best-of-many risk onto the FB4 stage that leaks at 20.6% — the net loosening the spec forbids (though it is opt-in default-off and otherwise single-chokepoint-guarded with regime kept HARD). The verdict floor direction (t_star only raises, never loosens) and UNEVALUATED-thin path are correct. Recommend: switch to fixed m=BUDGET (or a genuine alpha-spending rule), make the ledger persistent for the per-user scope to mean anything (or drop the cross-run/per-user claims), add the spec-mandated FWER simulation test, and wire MON1 before honoring soft_dsr.

findings.title: "Sequential Šidák" (m=spent+1) does NOT control family-wise error — false-PASS ≈0.21 at the 50-test cap, not 0.05
findings.detail: service.py:120-130 `campaign_adjusted_t(tests_so_far)` sets the k-th terminal test's bar to alpha_k = 1-(1-0.05)^(1/k) (loop.py:727-730 passes m = campaign_tests()+1). This is not a valid FWER procedure. The FIRST test alone spends the entire family budget (k=1 → alpha=0.05, t*=1.645), and every later test adds MORE type-I error on top. Under all-nulls-true independent tests the family false-PASS rate is 1-0.95^(H_K) (H_K = K-th harmonic number): I confirmed analytically AND by Monte-Carlo (200k paths) that at the K=50 budget cap FWER ≈ 0.206 — over 4x the advertised CAMPAIGN_ALPHA_FAMILY=0.05 — and it keeps rising (0.29 at 500, →1 asymptotically). The docstring/config claim 'the family-wise false-PASS rate across them is held at CAMPAIGN_ALPHA_FAMILY' is false. The correct control is a FIXED m: every test uses m = CAMPAIGN_OOS_BUDGET (50) → constant t*=3.083 → FWER=0.0499 (verified), conservative below the cap; or a genuine alpha-spending sequence whose per-test alphas SUM to ≤0.05 (the current ones sum like 0.05·ln K and diverge). This is the mechanism's central promise and it is not met. Because RT2 soft-DSR is explicitly justified as 'backstopped by the FB4-controlled lockbox', the soft-DSR loosening rides on a backstop leaking at ~21%, compounding the loosening.

findings.title: Default in-memory ledger makes the campaign cap per-RUN, defeating 'volume can never buy more lottery tickets'
findings.detail: run.py:143 defaults `oos_db_path=":memory:"` and router.py:437-465 never passes oos_db_path, so via the API the OOSLockboxService (and its oos_campaign_ledger) is a fresh in-memory DB PER RUN. Consequences: (1) the config JSON (docs/design/coverage-v2-gate-config.json) asserts scope 'per user … ledger persisted in oos_lockbox.db' — false under the shipped wiring; the per-user scope key str(user_id) is inert because each run only ever contains one run's tests. (2) A user circumvents the entire FB4 cap by starting a new run: each run restarts at tests_terminal=0, granting a fresh 50-test budget AND resetting the Šidák sequence to the loosest bar (1.645). So 'more volume can never buy more lottery tickets against the same hold-out data' holds only WITHIN one run; across runs volume buys unlimited fresh tickets. The stated FB4 goal ('campaign-wide … as lineage count scales') is delivered only within a single process invocation, not across the user's campaign. Either wire a persistent per-user oos_db_path or correct the config's persistence/scope claims.

findings.title: Campaign BudgetExhaustedError is swallowed as a generic ERROR, not routed through the spec's UNEVALUATED path
findings.detail: When the campaign cap trips, evaluate() (service.py:211-214) raises BudgetExhaustedError; _run_oos_lockbox does not catch it, so it propagates to loop.py:1283 `except Exception as exc: logger.error("OOS lockbox error", ...)`. _record_oos is never called. Vs. the config's stated 'depletion → BudgetExhaustedError → UNEVALUATED path': the candidate is NOT recorded/emitted as UNEVALUATED — no oos_result event is produced, so in the UI it is indistinguishable from a candidate OOS never reached, and every post-depletion candidate emits a spurious ERROR log line for a by-design condition (masking genuine OOS errors). Fix: catch BudgetExhaustedError from the campaign cap specifically in _run_oos_lockbox and call _record_oos(..., UNEVALUATED, ...) at info level. (No wasted backtest — the cap is checked before run_oos_backtest — and it is per-new-candidate, not an infinite retry of one candidate, so this is contract/observability, not a hot loop.)

findings.title: soft_dsr guard is construction-time only; after campaign depletion DSR stays SOFT with no functioning backstop
findings.detail: run.py:305-307 computes `_soft_dsr = soft_dsr and enable_oos and mode=='robustness'` once at gatekeeper construction. It cannot observe runtime depletion: once the campaign budget is exhausted, every OOS evaluation returns honest-absence (BudgetExhaustedError → no verdict), yet DSR remains SOFT for the rest of the run. Candidates past the cap therefore face NEITHER a hard DSR gate NOR a functioning hold-out judge — exactly the 'RT2 in SOFT while FB4 (effectively) inactive' state the spec forbids. Bounded (no OOS PASS badge is issued post-depletion, so nothing is mis-surfaced as validated), but the DSR integrity relaxation genuinely persists without the backstop that justified it. Consider treating a depleted campaign as FB4-inactive for the soft-DSR guard.

findings.title: Campaign floor hardcoded 1.645 disagrees with the real single-test bar VALIDATE_T=1.65
findings.detail: campaign_adjusted_t floors at a literal 1.645 (service.py:130) and the docstring/config call 1.645 'the single-test bar', but the actual OOS single-test bar is loop.py:268 VALIDATE_T=1.65. Harmless today because _oos_verdict (loop.py:575) clamps `eff_t = t_star if t_star>VALIDATE_T else VALIDATE_T`, so the m=1 value 1.645 is silently raised to 1.65. But the two constants disagree, the freeze test pins the 1.645 side, and the lockbox floor will not track a change to VALIDATE_T — a latent drift trap. Derive the floor from VALIDATE_T rather than duplicating a slightly different literal.

findings.title: Tamper-freeze pins the mis-calibrated Šidák values, giving false assurance of correctness
findings.detail: test_gate_config_freeze.py pins campaign_adjusted_t(0)=1.645 and test_fb4_campaign.py pins m=10→2.568, m=50→3.083. Good against silent drift, but these pinned numbers encode the FWER-broken curve (blocker #1). The tamper-wire guards that the numbers don't CHANGE, not that they're correct; fixing FWER will (correctly) require rewriting these pins. Ledger atomicity (single-session read+increment+terminal-write, commit only on PASS/FAIL) and UNEVALUATED/exception spending nothing (return before commit → added row rolled back) both verified correct; get_result recover path returns before evaluate so it does not double-count — those are fine. create_all on init adds the new oos_campaign_ledger table to pre-existing db files (new table, not a new column) — migration fine.

result.summary: One BLOCKER: the FB4 'sequential Šidák' (per-test alpha_k = 1-(1-0.05)^(1/k), m = spent+1) does not control family-wise error. The first terminal test alone spends the full 0.05 family budget and each later test adds more; analytic + 200k-path Monte-Carlo both give family false-PASS ≈0.206 at the 50-test cap (→1 asymptotically), not the advertised 0.05. The correct fix is a fixed m = CAMPAIGN_OOS_BUDGET (constant t*=3.083, verified FWER=0.0499) or a properly-summing alpha-spending sequence. HIGH: the shipped wiring uses the default in-memory lockbox (router never sets oos_db_path), so the ledger is per-run — a user resets the whole 50-test cap and the loosest 1.645 bar by starting a new run, defeating the 'volume can't buy more lottery tickets' goal and contradicting the config's 'per user / persisted' claim. Two MEDIUM contract issues (depletion swallowed as an ERROR rather than the spec's UNEVALUATED path; soft-DSR guard is construction-time only so DSR stays SOFT after the backstop depletes). Atomicity, UNEVALUATED-spends-nothing, get_result recover, and create_all migration all verified correct.
